scholarly_notes.theological_transfer: In Is 59:17 GOD wears the armor because Israel failed. In Eph 6:10-18 the BELIEVER wears God's armor. The genitive 'tou Theou' is crucial: it's not the believer's armor ŌĆö it BELONGS to God and He GRANTS it. Jerome: 'to put on the armor of God = to put on the Lord Jesus Christ' (Rm 13:14).

breastplate_debate_4_positions.Calvin: Practical righteousness (holy life) ŌĆö 'not imputation, subject is blameless life'
breastplate_debate_4_positions.Hodge: Imputed righteousness of Christ ŌĆö 'infinite obedience and sufferings of the Son of God' (Phil 3:8-9)
breastplate_debate_4_positions.Henry: BOTH ŌĆö 'Imputed = against divine wrath. Implanted = against Satan's attacks'
breastplate_debate_4_positions.Jerome: Armor = Christ himself ŌĆö 'Christ is called both truth and righteousness ŌĆö he IS the belt and breastplate'

scholarly_notes.no_armor_for_the_back: JFB, Henry, and Wesley all note: 'There is no armor for the BACK.' Implication: NEVER turn your back to the enemy.
scholarly_notes.deuterocanonical_bridge: Wisdom 5:17-20 describes God arming CREATION (holiness=breastplate, judgment=helmet, justice=SHIELD, wrath=sword). The SHIELD is absent from Is 59 but PRESENT in Wisdom 5:19 ŌĆö argument for deuterocanonical mediation. Not in ARA dataset (Protestant translation).

earliest_witnesses.Clement_of_Rome_AD_99: Shoes = gospel of peace (earliest testimony, 1st century)
earliest_witnesses.Polycarp_AD_155: Arm ourselves with armor of righteousness

scholarly_notes.1CO_16_13_strongest_connection: 44-53 votes to EVERY verse in Eph 6:10-18. The single most connected verse to the entire armor passage in the crossrefs dataset.
